topic.name: Hurricane and Tropical Storm History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the meteorological histories, impacts, and characteristics of various hurricanes and tropical storms over different years and regions.

Document 6: The 1865 Atlantic hurricane season included two landfalling hurricanes, with one that caused over 325 deaths.
Another hurricane occurred in late September, before the final storm of the season developed north of Panama.
Document 7: The 2002 Pacific hurricane season was a slightly above average Pacific hurricane season that saw three tropical cyclones reach Category 5 intensity.

topic.name: Historic Religious Structures

topic.name: Greco-Persian Conflicts
topic.description: The documents discuss the series of military conflicts and political struggles between the Greek city-states and the Persian Empire during the Greco-Persian Wars, focusing on events like the Ionian Revolt and key battles.

Document 1: The Greco-Persian Wars were a series of conflicts between the Achaemenid Empire of Persia and Greek city-states that started in 499 BC and lasted until 449 BC.
The collision between the fractious political world of the Greeks and the enormous empire of the Persians began when Cyrus the Great conquered the Greek-inhabited region of Ionia in 547 BC.
Document 2: The Battle of Lade was a naval battle which occurred during the Ionian Revolt, in 494 BC.
The Ionian Revolt was triggered by the dissatisfaction of the Greek cities of Asia Minor with the tyrants appointed by Persia to rule them.

topic.name: Mathematical Models and Theories
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical models and theories applied in different fields, including number theory, evolutionary biology, mechanical systems, and meteorology.

Document 1: Sylvester 's sequence is an integer sequence in which each member of the sequence is the product of the previous members, plus one.
The sum of its reciprocals forms a series of unit fractions that converges to 1 more rapidly than any other series of unit fractions with the same number of terms.
Document 2: Signalling theory is a body of theoretical work examining communication between individuals, both within species and across species.
Mathematical models in which organisms advertise their condition to other individuals as part of an evolutionarily stable strategy are important for research in this field.
Document 3: The mobility analogy is a method of representing a mechanical system by an analogous electrical system.
By converting to an electrical representation, these tools in the electrical domain can be directly applied to a mechanical system without modification.
Document 7: A tropical cyclone forecast model is a computer program that uses meteorological data to forecast aspects of the future state of tropical cyclones.
